items.title: Oil Traders Crowd Around Cheap TACO Hedge as Iran War Escalates
items.summary: An unusual options trade is becoming a favored hedge against the risks of sudden U-turns in US President Donald Trump’s stance on Iran, a dynamic that has repeatedly whipsawed markets in recent months.

items.title: Turning Oil to Fuel Has Never Been More Profitable as Refining Margins Surge
items.summary: Profit margins for US refiners making gasoline and diesel from crude oil are shattering records, as fuel supply disruptions driven by war lift prices for refined products.

items.title: RT by @mfa_russia: 🎙️Dmitry #Polyanskiy at OSCE on 16 July: on hybrid war and crimes of Kiev • In 9–15 July: >250 civilians affected, 32 killed, >5,200 munitions at civilian targets. Attacks on buses, gas stations, children, medics. Strikes on Kursk NPP2, chief engineer of Zaporozhye NPP killed. Sabotage with Western drones foiled. • AFU attack vessels in Azov and Black Seas – sailors hit. Russia struck Odesa and Chernomorsk ports. On the ground: ~200 sq km liberated since early July, just 3 km left to Slavyansk and Kramatorsk. SMO continues until all goals achieved. • On MH17: We mourn the victims but reject politicisation. Allegations are unfounded, investigation biased. ICAO decision of 30 June 2025 is groundless. Russia challenged it at the ICJ on 18 Sep 2025, and on 29 June 2026 submitted a Memorandum with irrefutable evidence. • On hybrid threats: London has no moral right to lecture us – it was Britain that derailed Istanbul talks, staged Bucha, supplied Storm Shadow, supported cyberattacks, and imposed pirate sanctions. Real destabilisation of Europe comes from the British elite. 📄 Read the full text: http://shorturl.at/QNWkA

items.original_full_text: The US-Israel war with Iran was supposed to blow up energy markets, but that hasn’t happened yet. In this weekly documentary, Bloomberg Originals explains why.

items.summary:
Украинские силы продолжили кампанию дальних ударов по российским военным объектам и энергетической инфраструктуре в России 15 и 16 июля.

На геолоцированных кадрах, опубликованных 15 июля, видны пожары в направлении российской авиабазы Энгельс-2 после ударов украинских беспилотников по городу Энгельс в Саратовской области.[20] Губернатор Саратовской области Роман Бусаргин 15 июля подтвердил, что украинские беспилотники нанесли удар по Энгельсу, заявив, что в результате удара был нанесен ущерб неустановленной гражданской инфраструктуре.[21] Российский оппозиционный источник Astra сообщил 16 июля, что в ночь с 15 на 16 июля на военном аэродроме Энгельс-2 произошел пожар.[22] 16 июля компания Astra сообщила, что украинские силы также нанесли удар по резервуарному парку нефтебазы Славнефть-ЯНОС в Ярославской области, вызвав пожар.[23] В Astra отметили, что нефтебаза емкостью более 620 тысяч кубометров является основным логистическим узлом Ярославского НПЗ. Губернатор Саратовской области Михаил Евраев подтвердил, что украинские силы ночью атаковали Ярославскую область.[24]

Кампания Украины по ударам беспилотников, направленная на то, чтобы лишить Россию возможности обеспечивать логистику и транспортировать топливо в оккупированный Крым, вероятно, еще больше ослабляет и без того растянутую российскую противовоздушную оборону и ставит новые дилеммы в области защиты.

Официальный представитель ВМС Украины капитан 2-го ранга Дмитрий Плетенчук сообщил 16 июля, что операции украинских
items.original_full_text:
Ukrainian forces continued their long-range strike campaign against Russian military assets and energy infrastructure in Russia on July 15 and 16.

Geolocated footage published on July 15 shows fires in the direction of the Russian Engels-2 Air Base following Ukrainian drone strikes against Engels, Saratov Oblast.[20] Saratov Oblast Governor Roman Busargin confirmed on July 15 that Ukrainian drones struck Engels, claiming that the strike damaged unspecified civilian infrastructure.[21] Russian opposition source Astra reported on July 16 that a fire broke out at the Engels-2 military airfield overnight on July 15 to 16.[22] Astra reported on July 16 that Ukrainian forces also struck the storage tank farm at the Slavneft-YANOS oil tank farm in Yaroslavl Oblast, causing a fire.[23] Astra noted that the oil tank farm has a capacity of over 620,000 cubic meters and is the main logistics hub for the Yaroslavl Oil Refinery. Saratov Oblast Governor Mikhail Yevrayev confirmed that Ukrainian forces targeted Yaroslavl Oblast overnight.[24]

Ukraine’s drone strike campaign aimed at denying Russia the ability to sustain logistics and transport fuel to occupied Crimea is likely further degrading Russia’s already outstretched air defenses and imposing new protection dilemmas.

Ukrainian Navy Spokesperson Captain 2nd Rank Dmytro Pletenchuk reported on July 16 that Ukrainian Unmanned Systems Forces’ (USF) operations in the Azov Sea have paralyzed Russian fuel shipping and complicated Russian supply deliveries to occupied Crimea.[9] Pletenchuk stated that Ukrainian forces have expanded their drone strike campaign against Russian maritime supply routes to the Black Sea and are threatening Russian seaborne lines of communication (SLOCs), particularly the Novorossiysk-Bosphorus and Don-Bosphorus SLOCs. Pletenchuk noted that the Azov-Black Sea region accounts for up to 20 percent of the Russian crude oil exports and that the Novorossiysk Port in Krasnodar Krai is the largest seaport in Russia. Pletenchuk stated that Ukrainian forces are targeting Russian efforts to transport fuel via small river-to-sea tankers. Pletenchuk also noted that Russian forces are increasingly relying on SLOCs after Ukrainian forces started to effectively interdict Russian ground lines of communication (GLOCs) connecting occupied Crimea to Rostov Oblast via southern Ukraine. Pletenchuk stated that Russian forces failed to bolster air defenses along Azov-Black Sea SLOCs despite repeated Ukrainian coastal warnings about navigation restrictions in the area over the past three years. Pletenchuk noted that Russian forces will be unable to protect all vessels due to air defense shortages and that Russian warships will be in danger of Ukrainian strikes if they escort cargo ships.

Pletenchuk reported that Ukrainian strikes are disrupting Russia’s aviation fuel supplies to airfields in occupied Crimea and will likely lead to future aviation fuel shortages and impact Russia’s ability to defend the Black Sea region against Ukrainian drones. Pletenchuk stated that Russian forces are avoiding relying on the Kerch Strait Bridge for logistics because of previous Ukrainian operations against the bridge and concerns of Ukrainian strikes. Pletenchuk implied that Ukrainian forces reportedly have not targeted the bridge because Russian forces will need to rely on the bridge to withdraw from occupied Crimea to Russia.

Ukraine’s intensifying intermediate- and long-range strike campaign targeting Russian ground and maritime supply routes in the Azov-Black Sea region will likely continue to isolate occupied Crimea and disrupt Russian military logistics, likely degrading Russian offensive operations and impeding further Russian advances in southern Ukraine.[10]

ISW continues to assess that the increasing frequency, size, and depth of Ukraine’s intermediate- and long-range strike campaign, now expanding to include strikes in the Azov-Black Sea region, will continue forcing Russia to overstretch its air defenses across a wide swathe of territory.[11]

General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine

Operational update as of 10:00 p.m. on July 16, 2026, regarding the Russian invasion.

In total, 199 combat engagements have taken place since the start of this 24-hour period. The aggressor launched one missile strike, fired eight missiles, carried out 54 airstrikes using 171 guided aerial bombs, deployed 6,076 kamikaze drones for attacks, and carried out 2,041 shelling attacks on the positions of our troops and populated areas.

In the Northern Slobozhanshchyna and Kursk directions, the enemy carried out 49 shelling attacks on populated areas and our troops’ positions, one of which involved the use of a multiple launch rocket system.

Over the past 24 hours, in the Southern Slobozhansky direction, the enemy launched 15 assaults on the positions of our units near the settlements of Izbytske, Kutkivka, Khatne, Kozacha Lopan, Hoptivka, Radkivka, Vilcha, and Bereznyky.

In the Kupiansk sector today, the enemy launched one assault on the positions of the Defense Forces in the Kolisnykivka area.

Nine attempts by the invaders to advance were repelled in the Lyman sector in the areas of Novoselivka, Drobysheve, Novomykhailivka, and toward the settlements of Dibrova and Ozerne. Three firefights are currently ongoing.

In the Sloviansk sector, the Defense Forces successfully halted 23 attempts by the invaders to advance toward the settlements of Kryva Luka, Rai-Oleksandrivka, Piskunivka, Mykolaivka, and in the areas of Riznykivka and Zakitne.

In the Kramatorsk sector, Russian invaders carried out one attack toward Tykhonivka.

In the Kostiantynivka sector, the Defense Forces repelled 16 enemy assaults in the areas around the settlements of Kostiantynivka, Illinivka, Ivanopillia, Stepanivka, and toward Dovha Balka.

The enemy carried out 25 attacks in the Pokrovsk sector. The occupiers attempted to advance in the areas around the settlements of Dorozhne, Hryshyne, Novopidhorodne, Rodynske, Vasylivka, and Kotlyne, as well as toward the settlements of Vilne, Novyi Donbas, Myrne, Serhiivka, and Kucheriiv Yar. Three firefights are still ongoing.

According to preliminary estimates, 52 occupiers were eliminated and 23 were wounded today; one multiple-launch rocket system, one vehicle, three pieces of specialized equipment, and one enemy fuel and lubricant depot were destroyed. Four artillery systems, four vehicles, three pieces of specialized equipment, five UAV command posts, and 172 enemy personnel shelters were damaged. A total of 219 unmanned aerial vehicles of various types were destroyed or neutralized.

In the Oleksandrivka sector, the enemy launched two attacks toward Vorone.

In the Huliaipole sector, the Defense Forces successfully repelled 14 enemy attacks toward the settlements of Kosivtseve, Verkhnia Tersa, Tsvitkove, Hirke, and Vozdvyzhivka.

In the Orikhiv sector, the enemy launched eight assaults on the positions of our defenders in the areas of Bilohirya, Shcherbakiv, Mali Shcherbakiv, and toward Novoandriivka.

In the Dnipro sector, the enemy did not conduct any offensive operations.

In other sectors, there were no significant changes in the situation.

Let’s defeat the enemy and bring victory closer. Glory to Ukraine!

The Ukrainian government approved a series of personnel changes in the Ukrainian Cabinet of Ministers, and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky appointed an interim defense minister on July 16.

The Ukrainian Verkhovna Rada (Parliament) approved on July 16 Zelensky’s endorsement to appoint former state-owned energy company Naftogaz CEO, Serhiy Koretsky, as the Ukrainian prime minister to replace Yulia Svyrydenko.[1] The Rada approved the reappointment of the First Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Energy Denys Shmyhal and Finance Minister Serhiy Marchenko. [2] The Rada approved the appointment of Chief of Ukraine’s National Police General Ivan Vyhivskyi as the minister of internal affairs; McKinsey & Company partner Oleksandr Kravchenko as the minister of economy; acting Digital Transformation Minister Oksana Ferchuk as the minister of digital transformation; Mykolaiv Oblast Military Administration Head Vitaliy Kim as the minister of veterans affairs; former Deputy Chair of the Rada Committee on State Power Organization, Local Self-Government, Regional Development, and Urban Planning Vitaliy Bezgin as minister of communities, territories, and internally displaced persons; and Kyiv Oblast Administration Head Mykola Kalashnyk as minister of reconstruction, infrastructure, and transport.

Zelensky appointed on July 16 former acting head of the Ukrainian Security Service (SBU) Yevheny Khmara as the interim defense minister to replace Mykhailo Fedorov, but the Rada has not considered Khmara’s appointment at this time.[3] Fedorov stated that he rejected Zelensky’s offer to become a presidential adviser or to remain on Zelensky’s team in another unspecified manner.[4] Fedorov’s dismissal sparked large-scale protests in cities across at least 18 Ukrainian cities, including in Kyiv, Lviv, and Kharkiv.[5] Former Ukrainian Ministry of Defense (MoD) advisor on defense technology and drone and electronic warfare (EW) expert Serhiy “Flash” Beskrestnov announced on July 15 that he will no longer assist the Ukrainian MoD following Fedorov’s dismissal.[6]

Former adviser to the Ukrainian defense minister Serhii Sternenko also announced on July 15 that he no longer works at the Ukrainian MoD.[7] Ukrainian Air Force Deputy Commander Colonel Pavlo Yelizarov announced on July 16 his resignation following Fedorov’s dismissal.[8]

items.title: В Одессе два человека погибли в результате ночного ракетного удара России по жилому дому. Еще пять человек получили ранения, в том числе трое детей. Все пострадавшие получают необходимую медицинскую помощь. Мои соболезнования их семьям и близким. Против Сумщины было использовано пятнадцать управляемых авиабомб. В Сумах пострадал один человек, повреждены жилые дома и гражданская инфраструктура, в том числе библиотека. Другие наши регионы также подверглись нападению. В Запорожской области в результате авиаудара два человека погибли и еще пятеро получили ранения. В Харьковской области в результате обстрела пострадали три человека. Один человек пострадал в Черниговской области. Под удар также попали Херсонская, Донецкая и Днепровская области. Всего за ночь противник запустил по Украине более 130 ударных беспилотников и восемь ракет. Важно, чтобы каждый государственный институт и все, кто участвует в переговорах с партнерами по поддержке обороны и устойчивости Украины, действовали с максимальной скоростью и эффективностью. Необходимо ускорить реализацию всего, что согласовано на уровне лидеров. Каждый оборонный комплекс сегодня – это не просто цифра, а конкретное оборудование, которое каждый день защищает жизни здесь, в Украине. Я благодарю всех, кто так работает, а также всех наших партнеров, которые помогают Украине.
items.original_title: In Odesa, two people were killed as a result of Russia’s overnight missile strike on a residential building. Five more people were injured, including three children. Everyone affected is receiving the necessary medical care. My condolences to their families and loved ones. Fifteen guided aerial bombs were used against the Sumy region. One person was injured in Sumy, and residential buildings and civilian infrastructure, including a library, were damaged. Our other regions also came under attack. In the Zaporizhzhia region, two people were killed and five more were injured in an air strike. In the Kharkiv region, three people were injured as a result of shelling. One person was injured in the Chernihiv region. The Kherson, Donetsk, and Dnipro regions also came under attack. In total, the enemy launched more than 130 attack drones and eight missiles against Ukraine overnight. It is important that every state institution and everyone involved in negotiations with partners on supporting Ukraine’s defense and resilience act with maximum speed and effectiveness. The implementation of everything agreed at the leaders’ level must be accelerated. Every defense package today is not just a number, but specific equipment that protects lives here in Ukraine every day. I thank everyone who works this way, as well as all our partners who are helping Ukraine.
